title: Data Governance & Audit Operations
decision_rationale: GL-209 establishes a bounded data-governance and audit-operations baseline for Developer Preview / Controlled Preview. It documents the no-go real/private data boundary, preserves immutable audit source history, adds a safe derived audit export check, and records remaining blockers without claiming production or compliance readiness.

current_state_summary.posture: Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries
current_state_summary.audit_source_history: append-only immutable baseline with hash-chain verification

data_classification_baseline.synthetic_demo_data: allowed in Developer Preview / Controlled Preview
data_classification_baseline.evidence_bundles: synthetic/demo only unless future gates approve otherwise
data_classification_baseline.operator_admin_metadata: allowed for controlled preview; tokens and hashes are sensitive
data_classification_baseline.tenant_workspace_identifiers: allowed as baseline identifiers; not production-complete isolation
data_classification_baseline.runtime_config_metadata: safe summaries only; raw secret values forbidden

retention_policy_baseline.synthetic_demo_data: may be reset/destroyed in ephemeral environments
retention_policy_baseline.real_private_data_retention: deferred because real/private data is no-go

deletion_policy_baseline.operational_non_audit_deletion: future controlled feature unless already implemented
deletion_policy_baseline.secret_rotation: must not require audit history mutation

audit_immutability_model.model: immutable source with derived safe exports
audit_immutability_model.failed_verification: incident signal, not destructive repair task

backup_restore_governance_alignment.backup_artifacts: must not contain secrets or real/private data
backup_restore_governance_alignment.restore_drill: must verify audit hash-chain and tenant/workspace separation where applicable

postgres_backup_restore_status.status: manual checklist only for ephemeral/synthetic instances

live_postgres_validation_status.status: not executed unless safe ephemeral instance is explicitly available and env-gated

data_governance_risk_register.risk: Derived exports could leak sensitive fields
data_governance_risk_register.mitigation: Export only safe manifests; redact/refuse secret-like content

data_governance_risk_register.risk: Deletion pressure could lead to source audit mutation
data_governance_risk_register.mitigation: Keep audit source immutable; redaction is derived-only

data_governance_risk_register.risk: Backup artifacts could include private data in future pilots
data_governance_risk_register.mitigation: Real/private data remains no-go until production governance exists

data_governance_risk_register.risk: Hash-chain failure could be mishandled as repair
data_governance_risk_register.mitigation: Treat as incident and investigate without destructive rewrite

data_governance_risk_register.risk: Baseline could be overclaimed as compliance readiness
data_governance_risk_register.mitigation: Explicitly prohibit compliance certification and GDPR/SOC2/ISO readiness claims

controlled_preview_impact.expansion: no expansion to real customer/private grant/institutional data

remaining_blockers: Production data governance policy and approval workflow
Production audit export authorization, encryption, and access logging
Production backup encryption, retention, offsite storage, and restore RTO/RPO
Live PostgreSQL validation execution against an ephemeral instance
Production observability and incident response program
Production IAM, tenant administration, and workspace enforcement
findings: Existing audit hash-chain verification is read-only.
Redaction belongs in derived exports/reports, not source audit mutation.
No destructive audit deletion or source audit redaction mutation was added.
No OpenAPI, migration, backend source, frontend, workflow, package, SDK, or public publish change was required.
recommended_next_issues: GL-206B Live PostgreSQL Validation Execution when ephemeral PostgreSQL is available
